questions.questionText: Line $L_1$ passes through the point $(1,2,3)$ and is parallel to $z$-axis. Line $L_2$ passes through the point $(\lambda, 5,6)$ and is parallel to $y$-axis. Let for $\lambda=\lambda_1, \lambda_2, \lambda_2<\lambda_1$, the shortest distance between the two lines be 3 . Then the square of the distance of the point $\left(\lambda_1, \lambda_2, 7\right)$ from the line $L_1$ is
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let a line passing through the point $(4,1,0)$ intersect the line $\mathrm{L}_1: \frac{x-1}{2}=\frac{y-2}{3}=\frac{z-3}{4}$ at the point $A(\alpha, \beta, \gamma)$ and the line $\mathrm{L}_2: x-6=y=-z+4$ at the point $B(a, b, c)$. Then $\left|\begin{array}{lll}1 & 0 & 1 \\ \alpha & \beta & \gamma \\ a & b & c\end{array}\right|$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let P be the foot of the perpendicular from the point $\mathrm{Q}(10,-3,-1)$ on the line $\frac{x-3}{7}=\frac{y-2}{-1}=\frac{z+1}{-2}$. Then the area of the right angled triangle $P Q R$, where $R$ is the point $(3,-2,1)$, is
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: For $$\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b} \in \mathbb{Z}$$ and $$|\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{b}| \leq 10$$, let the angle between the plane $$\mathrm{P}: \mathrm{ax}+y-\mathrm{z}=\mathrm{b}$$ and the line $$l: x-1=\mathrm{a}-y=z+1$$ be $$\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)$$. If the distance of the point $$(6,-6,4)$$ from the plane P is $$3 \sqrt{6}$$, then $$a^{4}+b^{2}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $$\mathrm{P}$$ be the plane passing through the line

$$\frac{x-1}{1}=\frac{y-2}{-3}=\frac{z+5}{7}$$ and the point $$(2,4,-3)$$.

If the image of the point $$(-1,3,4)$$ in the plane P

is $$(\alpha, \beta, \gamma)$$ then $$\alpha+\beta+\gamma$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\mathrm{P}_{1}$$ be the plane $$3 x-y-7 z=11$$ and $$\mathrm{P}_{2}$$ be the plane passing through the points $$(2,-1,0),(2,0,-1)$$, and $$(5,1,1)$$. If the foot of the perpendicular drawn from the point $$(7,4,-1)$$ on the line of intersection of the planes $$P_{1}$$ and $$P_{2}$$ is $$(\alpha, \beta, \gamma)$$, then $$\alpha+\beta+\gamma$$ is equal to ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the line $$\frac{2-x}{3}=\frac{3 y-2}{4 \lambda+1}=4-z$$ makes a right angle with the line $$\frac{x+3}{3 \mu}=\frac{1-2 y}{6}=\frac{5-z}{7}$$, then $$4 \lambda+9 \mu$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\mathrm{d}$$ be the distance of the point of intersection of the lines $$\frac{x+6}{3}=\frac{y}{2}=\frac{z+1}{1}$$ and $$\frac{x-7}{4}=\frac{y-9}{3}=\frac{z-4}{2}$$ from the point $$(7,8,9)$$. Then $$\mathrm{d}^2+6$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: One vertex of a rectangular parallelopiped is at the origin $$\mathrm{O}$$ and the lengths of its edges along $$x, y$$ and $$z$$ axes are $$3,4$$ and $$5$$ units respectively. Let $$\mathrm{P}$$ be the vertex $$(3,4,5)$$. Then the shortest distance between the diagonal OP and an edge parallel to $$\mathrm{z}$$ axis, not passing through $$\mathrm{O}$$ or $$\mathrm{P}$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the mirror image of the point (2, 4, 7) in the plane 3x $$-$$ y + 4z = 2 is (a, b, c), then 2a + b + 2c is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $${P_1}:\overrightarrow r \,.\,\left( {2\widehat i + \widehat j - 3\widehat k} \right) = 4$$ be a plane. Let P2 be another plane which passes through the points (2, $$-$$3, 2), (2, $$-$$2, $$-$$3) and (1, $$-$$4, 2). If the direction ratios of the line of intersection of P1 and P2 be 16, $$\alpha$$, $$\beta$$, then the value of $$\alpha$$ + $$\beta$$ is equal to ________________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let d be the distance between the foot of perpendiculars of the points P(1, 2, $$-$$1) and Q(2, $$-$$1, 3) on the plane $$-$$x + y + z = 1. Then d2 is equal to ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The angle between the straight lines, whose direction cosines are given by the equations 2l + 2m $$-$$ n = 0 and mn + nl + lm = 0, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Consider the lines L1: x - 1 = y - 2 = z and L2: x - 2 = y = z - 1. Let the feet of the perpendiculars from the point P(5, 1, -3) on the lines L1 and L2 be Q and R respectively. If the area of the triangle PQR is A, then 4A2 is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the equation of the line passing through the point $ \left( 0, -\frac{1}{2}, 0 \right) $ and perpendicular to the lines $ \vec{r} = \lambda \left( \hat{i} + a\hat{j} + b\hat{k} \right) $ and $ \vec{r} = \left( \hat{i} - \hat{j} - 6\hat{k} \right) + \mu \left( -b \hat{i} + a\hat{j} + 5\hat{k} \right) $ is $ \frac{x-1}{-2} = \frac{y+4}{d} = \frac{z-c}{-4} $, then $ a+b+c+d $ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The shortest distance between the lines $$\frac{x-4}{4}=\frac{y+2}{5}=\frac{z+3}{3}$$ and $$\frac{x-1}{3}=\frac{y-3}{4}=\frac{z-4}{2}$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If the equation of the plane containing the line

$$x+2 y+3 z-4=0=2 x+y-z+5$$ and perpendicular to the plane

$\vec{r}=(\hat{i}-\hat{j})+\lambda(\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k})+\mu(\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k})$

is $a x+b y+c z=4$, then $$(a-b+c)$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\lambda_{1}, \lambda_{2}$$ be the values of $$\lambda$$ for which the points $$\left(\frac{5}{2}, 1, \lambda\right)$$ and $$(-2,0,1)$$ are at equal distance from the plane $$2 x+3 y-6 z+7=0$$. If $$\lambda_{1} > \lambda_{2}$$, then the distance of the point $$\left(\lambda_{1}-\lambda_{2}, \lambda_{2}, \lambda_{1}\right)$$ from the line $$\frac{x-5}{1}=\frac{y-1}{2}=\frac{z+7}{2}$$ is ____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the line $$\frac{x}{1}=\frac{6-y}{2}=\frac{z+8}{5}$$ intersect the lines $$\frac{x-5}{4}=\frac{y-7}{3}=\frac{z+2}{1}$$ and $$\frac{x+3}{6}=\frac{3-y}{3}=\frac{z-6}{1}$$ at the points $$\mathrm{A}$$ and $$\mathrm{B}$$ respectively. Then the distance of the mid-point of the line segment $$\mathrm{AB}$$ from the plane $$2 x-2 y+z=14$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The shortest distance between the lines $${{x + 2} \over 1} = {y \over { - 2}} = {{z - 5} \over 2}$$ and $${{x - 4} \over 1} = {{y - 1} \over 2} = {{z + 3} \over 0}$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let a line $$l$$ pass through the origin and be perpendicular to the lines

$$l_{1}: \vec{r}=(\hat{\imath}-11 \hat{\jmath}-7 \hat{k})+\lambda(\hat{i}+2 \hat{\jmath}+3 \hat{k}), \lambda \in \mathbb{R}$$ and

$$l_{2}: \vec{r}=(-\hat{\imath}+\hat{\mathrm{k}})+\mu(2 \hat{\imath}+2 \hat{\jmath}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}), \mu \in \mathbb{R}$$.

If $$\mathrm{P}$$ is the point of intersection of $$l$$ and $$l_{1}$$, and $$\mathrm{Q}(\propto, \beta, \gamma)$$ is the foot of perpendicular from P on $$l_{2}$$, then $$9(\alpha+\beta+\gamma)$$ is equal to _____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The shortest distance between the lines $$\frac{x+7}{-6}=\frac{y-6}{7}=z$$ and $$\frac{7-x}{2}=y-2=z-6$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the plane $$P$$ passes through the intersection of two mutually perpendicular planes $$2 x+k y-5 z=1$$ and $$3 k x-k y+z=5, k<3$$ and intercepts a unit length on positive $$x$$-axis, then the intercept made by the plane $$P$$ on the $$y$$-axis is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the line $$\frac{x-3}{7}=\frac{y-2}{-1}=\frac{z-3}{-4}$$ intersect the plane containing the lines $$\frac{x-4}{1}=\frac{y+1}{-2}=\frac{z}{1}$$ and $$4 a x-y+5 z-7 a=0=2 x-5 y-z-3, a \in \mathbb{R}$$ at the point $$P(\alpha, \beta, \gamma)$$. Then the value of $$\alpha+\beta+\gamma$$ equals _____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Three lines $${L_1}:r = \lambda \widehat i$$, $$\lambda $$ $$ \in $$ R,

$${L_2}:r = \widehat k + \mu \widehat j$$, $$\mu $$ $$ \in $$ R and

$${L_3}:r = \widehat i + \widehat j + v\widehat k$$, v $$ \in $$ R are given.

For which point(s) Q on L2 can we find a point P on L1 and a point R on L3 so that P, Q and R are collinear?
questions.subject: Mathematics
